title: Disperma affine
taxonomy: Disperma affine is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Stilbaceae. It is native to South Africa and is known to grow in dry, rocky areas. The plant's classification is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, with the rank of species being the most specific classification for Disperma affine. The genus Disperma is also classified within the family Stilbaceae, indicating a close relationship with other species within this group. Overall, the classification of Disperma affine provides insight into the plant's evolutionary history and relationship to other members of the Stilbaceae family.

intro: Disperma affine is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Menispermaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, malaria, and digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Disperma affine include alkaloids, phenolic compounds, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects.
